{{Infobox character
| name = Divine Titans
| appears_in = The Last Horizon
| abilities = Combat Arts
| introduced = lh-02-the-engineer
}}

''See also: [[Divine Titans Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Divine [[Titans]] are powerful, massive combat machines that serve as essential weapons for fighting threats like the D'Niss. These masterpieces of engineering are designed to amplify a pilot's innate abilities, though they lack the adaptive systems found in [[Zenith Devices]]. To summon one, a pilot must use a specific spoken incantation that functions much like a spell; while the full phrase can be used, it requires intense concentration that leaves the user unable to focus on other tasks. Operating a [[Divine Titan]] is complex and carries significant risks. Pilots must maintain high levels of compatibility and teamwork to use them effectively, and some units require external assistance from a [[Captain]] to be controlled. If a [[Titan]] becomes unstable—such as when one was contaminated by the blood of a solar dragon—it can go on a rampage, potentially causing massive destruction to entire colonies. These machines come in various colors and configurations, including a pristine green model equipped with multiple integrated weapons and a scarlet unit marked by golden veins. While they are vital for protecting the innocent, their use is often a last resort due to the danger it poses to the pilot and the surrounding environment.}}

== The Engineer ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Divine Titans]] can be called while inside [[Subspace]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c9">''The Engineer'', chapter 9</ref>}}

== The Knight ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The Divine [[Titans]] are a group of five powerful entities originally invented and built by [[Doctor Janbell Cryce]]. These summoned beings are piloted by combatants and possess the unique ability to convey [[Combat Arts]]. Despite their immense strength, the Divine [[Titans]] are constrained by a limited energy capacity.}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Doctor Janbell Cryce]] is the inventor of the [[Divine Titans]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c2">''The Knight'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Doctor Cryce]] invented and built the [[Divine Titans]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c10">''The Knight'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Divine Titans]] are piloted by combatants.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c12">''The Knight'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Divine Titans]] can convey [[Combat Arts]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c12" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|There are five [[Divine Titans]] in total.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c20">''The Knight'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Divine Titans]] are entities that can be summoned.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c31">''The Knight'', chapter 31</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Divine Titans]] are powerful entities.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c39">''The Knight'', chapter 39</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[Divine Titans]] have a limited energy capacity.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c42">''The Knight'', chapter 42</ref>}}

== The Pilot ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Two [[Divine Titans]] emerged to support the [[Zenith]] Starship.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c26">''The Pilot'', chapter 26</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Red]] and green [[Divine Titans]] are fighting in the distance.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c29">''The Pilot'', chapter 29</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Divine Titans]] are kept in a hangar.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c32">''The Pilot'', chapter 32</ref>}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|“We are restricted by the energy capacity of the Divine Titans,” Shyrax noted.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c42" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:The Engineer]]
[[Category:The Knight]]
[[Category:The Pilot]]
[[Category:The Last Horizon]]
[[Category:The Last Horizon characters]]
